creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: GPU Cloud Deployment Without Leaving Your IDE — Audry Hsu, RunPod
videos.summary: Flash is a deployment tool that replaces complex Docker and GPU allocation workflows with a single decorator. Users add @flash.endpoint to Python functions for direct IDE-to-GPU-cloud deployment with hot reload and per-request pricing via RunPod's infrastructure.

videos.title: RAG is dead, right?? — Kuba Rogut, Turbopuffer
videos.summary: Kuba Rogut argues RAG is not dead despite social media discourse suggesting otherwise, citing Cursor's semantic search gains of 24% answer accuracy and 2.6% code retention, plus surging Google search volume for RAG in mid-2025. He reframes RAG as a broader retrieval paradigm combining vector search, full text search, regex, and filters iteratively, not just vector search alone, and characterizes embeddings as cached compute justified by query volume.

videos.title: From Transcription to Live Music: Gemini's Audio Stack — Thor Schaeff, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Gemini 3 Flash Preview provides audio understanding via a single API call with speaker labels, timestamps, emotion tags, language detection, translation, and summarization. The system also enables speech generation directed by text prompts and real-time multimodal audio processing through Gemini 3.1 Flash Live with integrated reasoning.

videos.title: 2026 AI Engineer Vibe Reel
videos.summary: The AI Engineer channel is promoting a World's Fair event scheduled for June 29 to July 2 in San Francisco. The post invites viewers to get tickets and view the event schedule.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live AI Q&A session on Saturday covering topics like AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The stream will feature discussion and chess gameplay, with regular content released on Wednesday evenings.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
videos.summary: Cole Medin presents a multi-model orchestration workflow where Claude Opus handles planning and reasoning while Gemini 3.5 Flash specializes in frontend design and UI generation. The approach composes specialized models to reduce cost while maintaining quality by routing each phase to the best-performing model.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: One Workflow for Beautiful Frontends (LIVE)
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates a workflow that chains Claude Opus for planning and integration, Gemini 3.5 Flash for UI design, and deployment using Archon as the orchestration framework. The approach leverages each model's strengths: Opus for reasoning and copy, Gemini for visual design, resulting in rapid frontend development.

videos.title: Harness Engineering: What Separates Top Agentic Engineers Right Now
videos.summary: Harness engineering is the practice of building control and orchestration layers around LLM-based coding agents. It operates at two levels: designing rules, skills, hooks, and subagents for individual sessions, and orchestrating multiple focused agent sessions into larger workflows to reliably scale task execution.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video explains how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving company systems through autonomous feedback loops. Covers construction of proactive AI loops, autonomous optimization use cases like ads and SEO, and tools like Loopany, memory systems, and tool integration enabling this architecture.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature for autonomous coding workflows, comparing it to Ralph-loop 2.0. The video covers goal-setting practices, mission exploration for long horizontal tasks, and demonstrates tools like Crewlet and Superdesign for building AI agents.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: AI Jason demonstrates OpenAI Symphony, a new AI coding paradigm that includes harness setup, auto testing capabilities, and workflow integration. The video covers how Symphony works and includes practical setup instructions.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason reviews recent advances in autonomous agents and self-evolving AI systems. The video covers autoresearch capabilities, Claude's multi-layer memory architecture, Hermes self-evolution mechanisms, and comparative analysis of Openclaw's self-skill development.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax releases M3, a language model implementing Sparse Attention (MSA) instead of full attention for improved inference efficiency. The model includes tiled I/O improvements and optimized KV cache reading to reduce latency during prefill and decode stages.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: A video explanation of Pi, an agent framework that enables novel use cases like OpenClaw. The video compares Pi's architecture and harness layer design against frontier agent tools including Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code reviews Huawei's announcement of the Tau Scaling Law as a competitive response in the China-USA semiconductor race. The video discusses how EUV and DUV lithography, packaging, and logic folding are becoming critical factors as the industry advances toward smaller node sizes.

videos.title: Agent Harness explained in 8min..
videos.summary: A video explanation of agent harness engineering and how it evolved from prompt engineering and context engineering. The video covers the limits of earlier approaches, harness engineering as a design pattern, agent loops, and overall architecture for agentic AI systems.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Host any coding agent on AgentCore
videos.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore enables hosting and execution of multiple coding agents including Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and Kiro. This centralized platform allows developers to deploy various coding agents on AWS infrastructure.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS video outlines five production best practices for AI agents: streaming tool call events for responsive UIs, layered authentication (OAuth plus IAM isolation), type-safe contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails with cost budgets and model routing, and comprehensive observability with metrics and distributed tracing. Demonstrates patterns using TypeScript, AWS services, and Strands Agents SDK.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: Tutorial on Infrastructure as Code using AWS Cloud Development Kit with Python. Demonstrates building a serverless REST API by comparing manual AWS console setup versus automated CDK deployment, covering DynamoDB, Lambda, and API Gateway.

videos.title: Stop AI Hallucinations With These 5 Techniques
videos.summary: A video demonstrating five techniques to reduce AI hallucinations and token waste in agent systems: semantic tool selection to filter context, graph RAG for precise queries, multi-agent validation, symbolic guardrails via code hooks, and runtime steering for self-correction. Code examples use Strands Agents and AWS Bedrock.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: How Meta AI accidentally gave away Obama's instagram
videos.summary: Meta AI systems reportedly exposed former President Obama's Instagram account through an accidental disclosure. The incident highlights risks of uncontrolled information leakage from AI-powered recommendation or retrieval systems.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech explores whether Google engineers use "vibe coding" as a practice. The video compares code generation approaches across OpenAI Codex and Claude, then examines coding trends at Google from 2024 through 2026.

videos.title: CEO finds out tokens cost money
videos.summary: A YouTube video from Alberta Tech presents a sarcastic or humorous take on a CEO discovering that AI model usage incurs token-based costs. The framing suggests surprise or realization about the economic model underlying large language model consumption.

videos.title: He's a marketing "engineer"!? 🤨
videos.summary: Alberta Tech questions the validity of someone being called a "marketing engineer," suggesting skepticism about the title or role definition.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that product-market fit is signaled by customer pull (pricing inquiries) rather than passionate enthusiasm, and that authentic validation comes from embedded observation in customer workflows rather than formal interviews. He emphasizes distinguishing real PMF signals from founder delusion and betting on AI model trajectories rather than current capabilities.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs, argues that advertising rather than subscriptions is the viable monetization model for AI tools, having raised $26M on this thesis. He contends that the primary bottleneck for AI agents is not technological capability but human trust and adoption timelines.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid, founder of Chatbase, shares his bootstrapping playbook for reaching $10M ARR without external funding. The discussion covers strategies for hitting $1M ARR in 117 days, churn reduction, pricing experimentation, SEO and AI-enhanced onboarding strategies, and decision frameworks for scaling a product-led growth business.

videos.title: A CS Professor on Why Slow Learning Wins in the AI Era | CU Boulder, Tom Yeh
videos.summary: Tom Yeh, Associate Professor at CU Boulder, advocates teaching AI fundamentals by hand at human speed rather than adopting the latest tools. He argues that focusing on mathematical foundations creates enduring skills that survive industry hype cycles, and cautions against an "AI-native" approach that treats AI as a transformative agent rather than a tool shaped by human intention.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Unlock AI Agent Control: Gemma 4, Local Power & Your Questions Answer #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Gemma 4 and local AI control capabilities in a short-form video. The channel aims to serve AI builders and solicits audience questions for future content direction.

videos.title: AI is Going LOCAL! Edge AI & Local Agents Explained #shorts
videos.summary: A video discussing the rise of edge AI and local agents in data-sensitive industries like finance and healthcare. The content emphasizes benefits of secure, private, and powerful local AI deployment as an alternative to centralized approaches.

videos.title: Google Gemma 4: Local AI Power for Video & Robotics! #shorts
videos.summary: Google Gemma 4 is a local AI model enabling on-device inference for video understanding and robotics applications. The tool emphasizes privacy benefits and reduced latency through edge-based processing rather than cloud dependency.

videos.title: Gemma 4: AI Frontier Now ON YOUR LOCAL DEVICE! #shorts
videos.summary: Gemma 4 is an open-weight AI model designed to run on local hardware without cloud processing. The video promotes its capability to deliver cutting-edge AI capabilities directly on user devices.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
